Feature_Name: L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) Configuration via RRCReconfiguration

3GPP_Reference.Title: NR; Radio Resource Control (RRC); Protocol Specification, NG-RAN; Architecture and functional description

Feature_Description: This procedure describes how the gNB-CU prepares and sends an 'RRCReconfiguration' RRC message containing L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) Configuration Information Elements (IEs) to the UE. The LTM configuration data is based on information received in previous F1AP messages. The 'RRCReconfiguration' message is piggybacked within an F1AP 'DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message sent from the gNB-CU to the source gNB-DU. The source gNB-DU then forwards the 'RRCReconfiguration' message to the UE. Upon successful application of the configuration, the UE responds with an 'RRCReconfigurationComplete' RRC message to the source gNB-DU, which in turn forwards this response to the gNB-CU, piggybacked in an F1AP 'UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message.
Feature_Objectives: To configure L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) parameters at the UE.
To enable the gNB-CU to provide LTM configuration data to the UE via the gNB-DU.
To confirm the successful application of the LTM configuration by the UE to the gNB-CU.

RRC_States_And_Transitions.Trigger: gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration (e.g., based on MeasurementReport from UE).
RRC_States_And_Transitions.Impact: UE applies new LTM configuration, potentially preparing for or executing LTM.

RRC_Messages.Purpose: To configure L1/L2 Triggered Mobility (LTM) related parameters in the UE.

Key_IEs.IE_Definition:
RRCReconfiguration-IEs ::= SEQUENCE {
    radioBearerConfig                   RadioBearerConfig                                       OPTIONAL,   -- Need M
    secondaryCellGroup                  SetupRelease { SecondaryCellGroup }                     OPTIONAL,   -- Need M
    measConfig                          MeasConfig                                              OPTIONAL,   -- Need M
    lateNonCriticalExtension            OCTET STRING                                            OPTIONAL,   -- Need S
    nonCriticalExtension                RRCReconfiguration-v1530-IEs                            OPTIONAL
}

LTM-Config ::= SEQUENCE {
    ltm-CandidateList                   LTM-CandidateList                                       OPTIONAL,   -- Need R
    ltm-CSI-ReportConfigList            LTM-CSI-ReportConfigList                                OPTIONAL,   -- Need R
    ltm-CSI-ResourceConfigList          LTM-CSI-ResourceConfigList                              OPTIONAL,   -- Need R
    ltm-TCI-InfoList                    LTM-TCI-InfoList                                        OPTIONAL,   -- Need R
    ...
}
Key_IEs.Mandatory_or_Optional: Optional (assumed to be part of RRCReconfiguration-IEs via extension for LTM configuration)

RRC_Messages.Purpose: To confirm the successful completion of the RRC reconfiguration procedure by the UE.
To transfer an RRC message (e.g., RRCReconfiguration) from gNB-CU to gNB-DU over the F1AP interface.
To transfer an RRC message (e.g., RRCReconfigurationComplete) from gNB-DU to gNB-CU over the F1AP interface.

RRC_Procedures.Trigger_Condition: The gNB-CU determines to initiate LTM configuration, typically based on measurement reports (L3 measurement results) received from the UE, indicating the need for L1/L2 Triggered Mobility.
RRC_Procedures.Description: The gNB-CU prepares an RRCReconfiguration message, embedding the LTM Configuration Information Elements (IEs) based on LTM configuration data previously received or determined. This RRCReconfiguration message is then encapsulated within an F1AP 'DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message and sent to the source gNB-DU. The source gNB-DU forwards the RRCReconfiguration message to the UE. After successfully applying the new LTM configuration, the UE generates an 'RRCReconfigurationComplete' RRC message. This response is sent to the source gNB-DU, which subsequently forwards it to the gNB-CU, encapsulated in an F1AP 'UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message. This procedure supports both intra-gNB-DU and inter-gNB-DU LTM scenarios.

Security_And_Context.Security_Mode_Impact: The RRCReconfiguration message is integrity protected and ciphered by PDCP once AS security is activated, as it is transmitted on SRB1 or SRB2. The LTM configuration IEs within this message are thus also protected.
Security_And_Context.UE_Context_Changes: The UE's AS context is updated with the new LTM configuration. The gNB-DU's UE context is updated with LTM configuration ID mapping list and CSI resource configuration during the preparation phase (UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST/RESPONSE).

Interlayer_Interactions.Purpose: To transfer the RRCReconfiguration message from the gNB-CU to the gNB-DU for onward transmission to the UE.
To deliver the LTM configuration to the UE.
To acknowledge the successful reception and application of the RRCReconfiguration message by the UE.
To transfer the RRCReconfigurationComplete message from the gNB-DU to the gNB-CU.

Call_Flow.Message: DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER (RRCReconfiguration with LTM Configuration IEs)

Procedure_Implementation_Steps: 1. The gNB-CU prepares the 'RRCReconfiguration' RRC message, including the LTM Configuration IEs, based on LTM configuration data received in previous F1AP messages (e.g., from UE CONTEXT SETUP REQUEST/RESPONSE or UE CONTEXT MODIFICATION REQUEST/RESPONSE procedures for LTM candidate cell preparation).
2. The gNB-CU sends the 'RRCReconfiguration' message buffer, piggybacked in an F1AP 'DL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message, to the source gNB-DU.
3. The source gNB-DU forwards the received 'RRCReconfiguration' message to the UE.
4. The UE processes the 'RRCReconfiguration' message, applies the LTM configuration, and responds with an 'RRCReconfigurationComplete' RRC message to the source gNB-DU.
5. The source gNB-DU forwards the 'RRCReconfigurationComplete' message to the gNB-CU, piggybacked in an F1AP 'UL RRC MESSAGE TRANSFER' message.
